title: Podman struggles with string delimiters in env files
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

string quoting is shaky depending on the notation used (single or double quotes) particularly when passing env files.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. build a simple image with a distro base, its binary python3, django, apache, and mod_wsgi. Have a Django app in "app" in your build folder with basic settings. Downloading Django and `python manage.py startapp base` should work to mimic what I've deployed.  My complete Dockerfile is below.

```
FROM centos:latest
ENV PYTHON_VERSION=3.6
ENV HOME=/home/apache
RUN yum update -y \
	&& yum install --setopt=install_weak_deps=False -y \
	httpd \
	python3 \
	python3-pip \
	python3-mod_wsgi \
	python3-cryptography \
	git-core \
	&& yum clean all
	
# Direct access and error logs to stderr for the container. 
# Disable heartbeat module and suexec module.
# =============================================================================
RUN sed -i -r 's@Listen .*@Listen 8080@i' /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf \
	&& sed -i -r 's@CustomLog "logs/access_log" combin.*@CustomLog "/dev/stderr" combined@i' /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf \
	&& sed -i -r 's@Errorlog .*@Errorlog /dev/stderr@i' /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf \
	&& sed -i -r 's@LoadModule lbmethod_heartbeat_module .*@#LoadModule lbmethod_heartbeat_module modules/mod_lbmethod_heartbeat.so@i' /etc/httpd/conf.modules.d/00-proxy.conf \
	&& sed -i -r 's@LoadModule suexec_module .*@#LoadModule suexec_module modules/mod_suexec.so@i' /etc/httpd/conf.modules.d/00-base.conf

# Default site set to our wsgi site
# =============================================================================
RUN echo -e "\n\n\
WSGIPythonPath /home/apache\n\
<VirtualHost *:8080>\n\
     ServerName localhost\n\
     WSGIScriptAlias / /home/apache/base/wsgi.py\n\
     <Directory /home/apache/base>\n\
      <Files wsgi.py>\n\
         Require all granted\n\
      </Files>\n\
     </Directory>\n\
</VirtualHost>" >> /etc/httpd/conf.d/00-wsgi.conf

# Put Django app in a home folder belonging to apache.
# Pip install Django
# Make migrations and migrate.
# Collect static
# Create an admin.
# Remove the .env file from the saved container image
# =============================================================================
ADD app /home/apache/
COPY .env /home/apache/.env
RUN chown -R apache:apache /home/apache && chown apache:apache /home/apache/.env
RUN chmod 770 /run/httpd
RUN usermod -d /home/apache apache
USER apache
WORKDIR /home/apache
RUN mkdir /home/apache/.local
RUN pip3 --no-cache install --user -r /home/apache/requirements.txt
RUN export $(grep -v '#.*' /home/apache/.env | xargs) \
	&& python3 /home/apache/manage.py makemigrations \
	&& python3 /home/apache/manage.py migrate \
	&& python3 /home/apache/manage.py createsuperuser --noinput \
	&& python3 /home/apache/manage.py collectstatic --noinput \
        && rm -f /home/apache/.env

# Start Apache2 service in the foreground of the container
# =============================================================================
EXPOSE 8080
CMD ["httpd", "-D", "FOREGROUND", "-e", "info"]
```

2. declare DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE as a variable in a file in your build directory `.env` like so:
```    
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE='base.settings.dev'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_EMAIL='admin@admin.com'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD='admin'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_IS_ACTIVE='True'
```

Declare some other env vars for runtime in your Django settings, launch the image with the following:

`podman run -it -p 8080:8080/tcp --env-file=.env mod_wsgi-centos-image-name`

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

When starting the server you'll get the following error when trying to connect to it:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/84097090/127900138-89e32a4e-535c-4b58-bde1-5fe0920bcd65.png)



**Describe the results you expected:**

Single and double quoted strings in env files should behave as they do in shells. Currently they do not. I've identified the bug above where a single quoted string in an imported env file seems to pass along its single quote rather than using it as a delimiter, and I also cannot send an env file across into a Podman container at runtime with spaces or gt/lt, for instance:

`ADMIN_EMAIL='Admin <admin@admin.com>'`

The above is valid in bash on OSX, but Podman stops on the `<` and complains about an invalid string when receiving an env file imported with such a key/value argument.

It should be noted that Django declares `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE` in its wsgi entry script, and so declaring an environment variable containing the same value as the wsgi entry script to run shell commands within the framework is effectively overriding it, and the Django entry script uses double quote notation in a tuple.  Ex:

shell env version: `DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE='base.settings.dev'`
python django version: `os.environ.setdefault("DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE", "base.settings.dev")`


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman -v
podman version 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.6
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown'
  cpus: 1
  distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "11"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: debian
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.10.0-8-amd64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 446402560
  memTotal: 2079293440
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun'
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns'
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.0.1
      commit: 6a7b16babc95b6a3056b33fb45b74a6f62262dd4
      libslirp: 4.4.0
  swapFree: 1019072512
  swapTotal: 1022357504
  uptime: 5h 25m 30.82s (Approximately 0.21 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /home/media/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 1
    paused: 0
    running: 1
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: 'fuse-overlayfs: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs'
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.10.3
        fuse-overlayfs: version 1.4
        FUSE library version 3.10.3
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  graphRoot: /home/media/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 18
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/media/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.0.0
  Built: 0
  BuiltTime: Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.15.9
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.1
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
apt list podman
Listing... Done
podman/unstable,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+b1 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

I did check the release notes between the version I have and the next minor revision for notes on env files.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

VirtualBox running Debian Sid for building images, installed podman from the Debian unstable repo.

comments.body:
I am not sure what you see us doing wrong.
```
 docker run --env-file=/tmp/.env1 alpine printenv
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
HOSTNAME=b978fcc8f6b5
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE='base.settings.dev'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_EMAIL='admin@admin.com'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD='admin'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_IS_ACTIVE='True'
HOME=/root
```
Matches
```
$ podman run --env-file=/tmp/.env1 alpine printenv
PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
TERM=xterm
container=podman
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE='base.settings.dev'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_EMAIL='admin@admin.com'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD='admin'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_IS_ACTIVE='True'
HOME=/root
HOSTNAME=e4086977a938
```

Build box after importing the env file, in bash:

```
media@debian:~$ env
SHELL=/bin/bash
EMAIL_PORT=587
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_IS_ACTIVE=True
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD=admin
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_EMAIL=admin@admin.com
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE=base.settings.dev
```

Container after importing the env file, in bash:

```
media@debian:~$ podman run -it --env-file=.env mod_wsgi-centos
bash-4.4$ env
EMAIL_PORT='587'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD='admin'
DJANGO_SUPERUSER_EMAIL='admin@admin.com'
DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE='base.settings.dev'
```

The above are not the same, evidenced by the fact that the single quote delimiter in the value of DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE made it to Apache and caused the server error:

```
[wsgi:error] [pid 5:tid ...] [client 10.0.2.100:42052] ModuleNotFoundError: No module named "'base"
```
We are following the behavior of Docker.  So your environment file should not include the quotes.
